name: Prenatal Stretch & Mobility
description: A 25-minute active stretch and mobility session. Mix of dynamic warmup movements and deeper static holds targeting pregnancy trouble spots — hips, lower back, shoulders, and calves.

exercises.notes: Add hip circles between reps — figure-8 motion
Standing — reach overhead and bend to each side. Open the ribcage.
Slow, wide stance — pause 2 seconds at the bottom of each rep.
Pedal feet, bend one knee at a time. Active warmup for hamstrings and calves.
Each side — sit tall, lean forward. Try gentle pulses for 10 reps then hold.
Pulse knees 10x then hold. Lean forward from hips.
Each side — go deeper than you think. Use pillows if hip is far from floor.
Deep squat hold — elbows press knees wide. Rock gently side to side.
Each side — sit tall, lean forward. Important for sciatic relief.
Each side — gentle pull, opposite leg bent
Each side — pillow between knees. Modified side-lying if uncomfortable.
Gentle — press up just enough to feel the stretch. Avoid deep backbends.
Knees wide. Walk hands to the left, hold 15s. Walk to the right, hold 15s. Back to center.
Each side — foot on low surface, gentle lean. Keep back flat.
Each side — use wall for balance
Each side — straight leg then bent leg. Helps with nighttime cramps.
Each side — use a towel or strap. Side-lying modification available.

tips: The dynamic warmup should get blood flowing — don't skip it
Hip openers will feel more productive as pregnancy progresses and relaxin kicks in
Malasana shows up in every session for a reason — it's the best labor prep stretch
Piriformis stretch helps with sciatic pain — common in second trimester
Calf stretches can prevent nighttime leg cramps
Always consult your healthcare provider before starting any exercise program during pregnancy
